examples: But in the face of the American threat, the hardliners began clamping down capriciously.
Don't change your mind capriciously - being agile is not the same thing as lacking vision.
In the Mesopotamian stories, for example, the gods act capriciously, the gods act on a whim.
At least one ex-employee claims he was capriciously fired over a seemingly trifling disloyalty.
Scott, on the other hand, assembled his team capriciously, from old-friends, casual acquaintances, and hangers-on.
